description:
Add Buck2 rules for compiling Jsonnet files to JSON.

## Background
Jsonnet is a data templating language that extends JSON with variables, functions, conditionals, and imports. It's commonly used for:
- Kubernetes manifests
- Configuration files
- Infrastructure as code (Terraform, etc.)

Buck2's default prelude does not include Jsonnet support.

## Desired Rules

### jsonnet_library
Compile Jsonnet to JSON, exposing outputs for other rules to consume.
```starlark
jsonnet_library(
    name = "config",
    srcs = ["config.jsonnet"],
    deps = [":common"],  # Other jsonnet_library targets
    ext_strs = {"env": "prod"},  # --ext-str flags
    ext_codes = {"count": "3"},  # --ext-code flags
)
```

### jsonnet_to_json (or similar)
Generate JSON files from Jsonnet sources.
```starlark
jsonnet_to_json(
    name = "k8s-manifests",
    src = "deployment.jsonnet",
    deps = [":config"],
    multi = True,  # Multi-file output mode (-m)
)
```

## Toolchain
- Add `jsonnet` to registry (nixpkgs has it)
- Create `system_jsonnet_toolchain` rule
- Support both `jsonnet` and `go-jsonnet` implementations

## Implementation Notes
- Jsonnet has import resolution that needs careful handling
- JSONNET_PATH environment variable for library paths
- Consider how deps translate to import paths
- Multi-file output mode generates multiple JSON files

## References
- https://jsonnet.org/
- https://github.com/google/jsonnet
- https://github.com/google/go-jsonnet
title: Add Jsonnet rules to prelude extensions
